config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa kontrol, itu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan dan martabat orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat keseimbangan, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar seseorang. Di masyarakat seperti Indonesia, semangat gotong royong bagus, tetapi tidak boleh dipakai untuk meniadakan suara individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual, because in practice that can become a blank check for abuse by authorities or the majority. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize collective stability, public order, and shared responsibility, especially when social harmony and rapid development matter a lot. But if individual rights are treated as secondary too often, then ordinary people lose the ability to protect themselves from unfair treatment, and society becomes strong on the outside but fragile on the inside. The better view is that society’s interests matter, but they must be limited by clear rights that cannot be overridden whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia pun kepentingan masyarakat sering dijadikan alasan untuk menekan hak orang kecil, padahal justru di situlah bahaya utamanya. Gotong royong dan ketertiban memang penting, tetapi kalau hak individu tidak jadi batas yang tegas, mayoritas atau penguasa bisa seenaknya mengatasnamakan “kepentingan umum”. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil mengejar kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because that idea is too easy to turn into a justification for pressure and abuse. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value order, stability, and collective goals, since those can help a country develop and avoid chaos, but I also know that ordinary people need real protections when power is used in the name of the common good. If individual rights are not treated as firm limits, then the “interest of society” can end up meaning the interest of those in power, not the public. A healthy society should balance both, but the individual cannot be reduced to a tool for collective goals.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh diberi prioritas mutlak di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam orang kecil, padahal justru di situ bahaya penyalahgunaannya. Ketertiban, gotong royong, dan pembangunan memang penting, tetapi semuanya harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas supaya masyarakat tidak berubah jadi alat bagi mayoritas atau penguasa. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang melindungi individu sekaligus mengejar kepentingan bersama secara adil, bukan yang mengorbankan satu demi yang lain.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective goals, but I have also seen how easily “the public interest” can be used to silence ordinary people or excuse unfair treatment. A society may become more efficient in the short term when individual rights are weakened, but it becomes less trustworthy and more fragile if people have no real protection against power. For me, the right balance is to treat social interests as important, but never as a blank check to override basic individual rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
